feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition
feed.subtitle: SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Workzone - is it possible to merge content from different channels?
entry.content:
Hello,

I don't think this is possible but maybe I missed something on the documentation. Say you have a workzone instance (standard) with multiple channels configured (e.g. S/4 HANA and business solutions), and they contribute pages with the same names. Is it possible to merge them instead of having a duplication of tabs with the same name ?

 

Thanks in advance

 

@Vera_Gutbrod @ThomasReiss

entry.title: How do i trigger process automation(BPA) through custom ui5 application?
entry.content:
Hello experts, 
I am searching for "trigger build process automation using Custom ui5 application", i think we have to create destination to trigger it but i don't know the actual process.
please come up with suitable solution.
#BPA #Ui5 #automation #BTP #Fiori #SAP

Best regards
Kushagra Sharma

entry.title: Issue with Federated Application Links - SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
Hello!

We are encountering an issue with a custom UI5 application that integrates with an on-premise SAP application via application federation. The custom UI5 app contains a link to the federated application, which works as expected. However, the federated application itself contains additional links to other applications that we do not wish to federate. (and are not part of the federated role).

When a user clicks on the federated application link, the application attempts to load but results in a blank screen with no error message or indication of what might be going wrong so the user does not know what is happening.

  1. Is this behavior expected when attempting to access non-federated links from a federated application?
  2. Are there any configuration parameters or settings that can be applied to either the custom UI5 app or the federated application to prevent this issue? For example, can we restrict or block access to non-federated links?

entry.title: Passing parameters into ECSC?
entry.content:
I've built an HR site in Work Zone that offers guidance on topics for employees eg. taking leave. 

If an employee has a question about leave atm they have to open ECSC and select the category of their query from select boxes in a 2-tier category taxonomy.

I want ECSC to open with the correct parent and child category selected based on the user's point of origin. Eg. if they originated on the maternity leave page, the category select boxes Leave > Maternity leave should already be selected. This saves the user the task of navigating the select boxes. 

How can I pass the page of origin through to ECSC so it can do this?

entry.title: manifest file has two different application configured but only one tile appears in Build Workzone
entry.content:
Hi All,

Background: I have one sap ui5 application which launches two different application based on custom parameter.

Requirement : I want this application to be deployed in SAP Build Workzone as two separate applications.
There are two crossNavigation added to the manifest.json file of the project. 

Issue: However only one project shows up in SAP Build Workzone. Can someone suggest how to fix this.

"crossNavigation": {
"inbounds": {
"atom-ui-claims-inbound": {
"signature": {
"parameters": {
"appmode":{
"defaultValue": {
"value" : "claims"
}
}
},
"additionalParameters": "allowed"
},
"semanticObject": "Claims",
"action": "manage",
"title": "Claim Management",
"subTitle": "3.0",
"icon": "sap-icon://request"
},
"atom-ui-claimfinance-inbound": {
"signature": {
"parameters": { },
"additionalParameters": "allowed"
},
"semanticObject": "ClaimsBulk",
"action": "manage",
"title": "Claim Processing",
"subTitle": "Finance",
"icon": "sap-icon://my-sales-order"
}
}
}

entry.title: Identity Authentication Activation in Build Work Zone for SAP Joule (SUFA)
entry.content:
Hi together,
to activate SAP Joule in SuccessFactors (SUFA) as described in the Discovery Center mission, I need to enable Identity Authentication in SAP Build Work Zone. Unfortunately, I'm currently blocked due to access and configuration issues.

Here’s what has happened so far:

  • The SuccessFactors instance is successfully connected in a separate BTP Global Account.

  • I’ve subscribed to SAP Build Work Zone in my BTP Subaccount.

  • The trust to the IAS tenant is set up and active.

  • I manually created my user in IAS (with the same email address), but in the BTP cockpit, a second user appears – still without access to the “Identity Authentication” tab in Work Zone.

  • An admin account with access to the tenant also doesn’t see the necessary tab.

  • “Create Shadow Users During Logon” is enabled in the trust configuration.

I suspect that either the original service subscriber is not correctly linked in IAS, or there’s a mapping issue between users and the IAS setup.

Here’s the mission I’m working on:
https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/protected/index.html#/mymissiondetail/152454/card/11573701/?tab=projectboard

I’d appreciate support or a recommended workaround to proceed with the activation.

Thanks and best regards,
Lucas Maier

entry.title: Neo Site Migration | Import a SuccessFactors design page in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition
entry.content:
Hi everyone.

I have a NEO site which is based on page design from SuccessFactors and It looks like the image below.

 Other thing that I found is that there's a destination created for importing the custom pages, like the following.

 

Now I need to migrate this Neo site to BTP Foundry, but using "SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition" service. How can I do that?

Is it neccesary to have the "SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition" license?

Please your help. Thanks in advance.

entry.title: 403 Forbidden Error When Sending Email via SAP Cloud SDK from SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

I have developed and deployed an SAP BTP CAP application on the SAP BTP Cloud Foundry environment and integrated it with SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition.

I am using the SAP Cloud SDK (@SAP-cloud-sdk/mail-client) to send emails from my SAP BTP CAP application. The email functionality works perfectly when testing locally using SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) or when accessing the application directly in the Cloud Foundry environment in SAP BTP.

However, when I open the application and try to send an email through SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition, I encounter a 403 Forbidden error while sending an email.

I have already added the necessary routes in the xs-app.json file, but the issue persists.

Has anyone faced a similar issue? Are there additional configurations required for SAP Build Work Zone to allow outbound email requests?

Any insights or suggestions would be greatly appreciated!

xs-app.json:

xs-app.json
{
  "welcomeFile": "/index.html",
  "authenticationMethod": "route",
  "routes": [
 {
      "source": "^/MailService/(.*)$",
      "target": "/MailService/$1",
      "destination": "srv",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa",
      "csrfProtection": false
    }
  ]
}

srv > mail.cds:

@protocol: 'rest'
@path    : '/MailService'
service Catalog {

   @open
   type object {};
action sendemail();
}

Error:

Regards,
Syed

entry.title: Work Zone search results: content pages that also appear in custom nav appear twice
entry.content:
In my WZ site I have a workpage that also appears in the custom nav.

When I search for this page it appears in results twice - see attached. 

One result is listed as a "Workpage", which when clicked presents the page in it's workpage view, without the navigation I've built. I don't want this search result to display. In general I don't ever want to expose my users to the content directory or content view. This is not how web pages behave. 

The other, confusingly, is listed as "Workspace tabs", which displays the page with the page navigation. I DO want this result to appear. 

How can I control search results to prevent this pointless and distracting duplication?

Why does this even happen?

entry.title: How prevent Work Zone opening external links in a new tab
entry.content:
Work Zone always opens external links in a new tab. I don't want this, I want to keep users in one tab and preserve the unitary browsing experience, where the back button continues to behave as expected. 

How can I prevent external links opening in a new tab?

entry.title: Work Zone UI integration card: lose the Success Factors navbar?
entry.content:
I'm surfacing some data from Success Factors in my Work Zone site using UI integration cards. 

When I do this the card shows the data I want, but also the SF navigation bar at the top of the card - see attached, red box.  I've seen usability tests where users disappear into SF in the UI card window, fiddling about with the SF nav bar whilst trying to complete a task completely unrelated. It confuses and distracts them and I don't want to display it. 

How can I call the SF view into WZ without the SF navbar?

entry.title: Can I deliver a custom theme to all subscribers of my multitenant SaaS solution?
entry.content:
Hi Community,

how can a SaaS provider deliver a SAP custom theme (created with UI Theme Designer in Provider Subaccount) to the SaaS application of the subscriber.

The solution which is possible at the moment:
Develop a Launchpad Module in the CAP Project and integrate the theme designer to it. This allows to use a custom theme created by the customer/subscriber. But themes from provider are not selectable/usable.

Documentation for creating a launchpad module:
Create a Multitarget Application and Add Modules | SAP Help Portal

Documentation for using theme designer in launchpad module:
Creating Custom Themes | SAP Help Portal

UI theme designer SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

entry.title: Subscription Failed Error for SAP Build Work Zone in BTP Trial Account
entry.content: I am currently working with SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, in my BTP trial account. However, I've encountered a "Subscription Failed" error while attempting to subscribe to this application. The error message indicates that I need to link an Identity Authentication tenant to the subaccount with the "Establish Trust" option. I've attempted to subscribe multiple times, but the issue persists. I would appreciate your guidance on how to resolve this error. Thank you for your assistance!

entry.title: App-to-App navigation via Link/Popover on SAP Build Workzone with Fiori Elements
entry.content:
Hi Fiori Elements community,

I am developing a Fiori Elements List report application with a CAP OData v4 as the service layer.
I want to enable external app navigation via annotations to several other fiori applications. This is possible via the annotation UI.DataFieldForActionGroup and UI.DataFieldForIntentBasedNavigation

annotate service.Entity with @(
    UI.LineItem                    : [
        {
            $Type  : 'UI.DataFieldForActionGroup',
            Label  : 'Navigate To',
            Actions: [
                {
                    $Type          : 'UI.DataFieldForIntentBasedNavigation',
                    Action         : 'display',
                    SemanticObject : 'Object1',
                    RequiresContext: true,
                    Label          : 'App 1'
                },
                {
                    $Type          : 'UI.DataFieldForIntentBasedNavigation',
                    Action         : 'manage',
                    SemanticObject : 'Object2',
                    RequiresContext: true,
                    Label          : 'App 2'
                },
            ]
        },
    ],
)

 This will result in an action in the table toolbar

 

However I would like to get a column which will display a certain property of my entity as a link, e.g.:

This can be done with UI.DataFieldWithIntentBasedNavigation inside UI.LineItem. It would be great to assign several SemanticObject/Action combinations to this link, which are displayed in a Popover, so the user can choose to which app should be navigated.
I tried a combination with UI.DataFieldWithActionGroup and entries of UI.DataFieldWithIntentBasedNavigation but this is just ignored from the Fiori Elements framework.

Any help is greatly appreciated.

Thanks

entry.title: Issue with Automatic Theme Detection for Custom Themes in Build Workzone
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have two custom themes (light and dark) in the UI Theme Designer. I created a custom set so that the themes would automatically adjust based on the local system, similar to SAP Quartz (Set). However, the custom set is not appearing in the Build Workzone standard edition launchpad under User Menu > Settings > Appearance.

I have found that automatic theme detection is not supported for custom theme sets. If this is the case, what is the alternative way to achieve this feature?

Findings: 

What's New for SAP Business Technology Platform | SAP Help Portal

Regards,
Vishal Lekharajani

entry.title: Error Creating Content Provider for SAP Build Work Zone in Consumer Subaccount
entry.content:
I am trying to create a multi-tenant application with SAPUI5 interface that is consumed by the subscriber's SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition subscription, but I am getting errors trying to create the Content Provider. 

Following the tutorial in the documentation Developing HTML5 Business Solutions as Content Providers, which has steps for Multitenant consumption via subscription. I updated the "sap.cloud.service" in the SAPUI5 manifest.json file, added the cdm.json file and the script on the mta.yaml file to copy it to the resources folder. I also defined the destination templates in the provider side.

After that I followed the Administrator steps in Federation of HTML5 Business Solutions documentation. Subscribed to the multi-tenant app from another subaccount in the same environment and region. Exported and imported the destinations to the consumer subaccount, changing the runtime destination URL to the subscription URL of the consumer.

However after I create the Content Provider in the consumer SAP Build Work Zone channel manager, the content fetch fails with the following message:

"<Connectivity/InternalError>: Could not fetch content due to an internal error. Please try again later. If the issue persists, please open a ticket on the connectivity service."

This is the code I am currently working in https://github.tools.sap/I538205/simple-saas-mt/tree/bwz 
and I also tested the sample from https://github.com/SAP-samples/build-workzone-integration/tree/main/standard/html5-content-provider-sample which also failed to import the content provider.

Has anyone else here faced this issue? How can I solve it?

entry.title: Error 404 when trying to access a full stack CAP application deployed in Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I have created a full stack CAP application and deployed it in Cloud Foundry. However when trying to access the app via HTML5 repository, I get a blank page and when I open the console, I see a bunch of 404 errors.

 

I have followed a youtube tutorial: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=z10xke7yli4&list=PL54JdsBCvI0mPwzMkzlNnulw3NaxoWzks&index=23
The steps which I followed were exactly the same, still I am getting this issue.

Steps to reproduce:

1. Clone the github repository: https://github.com/developedbysom/cap-animal-adopt.git

2. Run npm install.

3. Run cds add hana --for production

4. Run cds add xsuaa --for production

5. Run cds add workzone (Since we are using managed approuter, this will also create xs-app.json in app/adopter)
6. Run npm install --prefix app/adopter/

7. Run cds add mta 
8. Run mbt build -t gen --mtar mta.tar && cf deploy gen/mta.tar

I have added my xs-app.json,mta.yaml and manifest.json files as txt in the question.
Please look into this and guide me if I am doing anything wrong.

Thanks,

Regards,

Raihan

entry.title: Performing actions from Work Zone notifications and handling it in CAP application
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I have followed the Developing Cloud Foundry Applications With Notifications tutorial and successfully created an application with notifications working as expected.

Now, I am trying to enhance it by adding actions to the notifications. I am currently facing an issue in the following scenario:

Use Case: Leave Request Action

  • I have a Leave Request entity.
  • When a leave request is created, a notification is sent to the approver.
  • The notification includes an action button – for example: "Reject Leave".
  • When the user clicks the Reject action, the backend CAP Java application receives a call to the /ExecuteAction API.

 

Problem:

In the /ExecuteAction call, I want to receive the Leave Request ID (or some unique record identifier) so that I can find and reject the correct record in the backend.

I tried adding a placeholder like {leaveRequestId} in the ActionId or TargetParameters, but this did not work.

What I Need Help With:

  • How can I pass the leave's record ID or other dynamic parameters to the backend via the /ExecuteAction payload?
  • Is there a supported way to include contextual data in the action definition or payload so that the backend knows which record to act on?

entry.title: SAP IAS Federated Access AWS Cognito
entry.content:
Hello SAP-Community,

In a recent project for a client, we had a feature request that consisted of making a Fiori application available in SAP BTP Workzone, so far so good. Currently, this client has a mobile application developed by a third party that uses AWS Cognito as an identity provider (IdP) and our need is to provide access to this Fiori application without a request new login.

My question would be whether it is possible to establish a trust relationship between SAP IAS and AWS Cognito so that a new login operation is not required? If so, is there any documentation or a step-by-step guide to help us?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: In Work Zone how do I exclude a page from search results?
entry.content:
In Work Zone I want to exclude a page from the search results. 

How can I do this?
